data.text: a fitted covering (as of leather or rubber) for the foot that usually reaches above the ankle
examples: gave the ball a good boot
got a big boot out of the joke
A cold boot is starting up a computer whose power has been turned off. A warm boot involves restarting the system while it is running …

data.text: something that resembles or is likened to a boot; especially: an enclosing or protective casing or sheath (as for a rifle or over an electrical or mechanical connection)
examples: gave the ball a good boot
got a big boot out of the joke
A cold boot is starting up a computer whose power has been turned off. A warm boot involves restarting the system while it is running …

data.text: an instrument of torture used to crush the leg and foot
examples: gave the ball a good boot
got a big boot out of the joke
A cold boot is starting up a computer whose power has been turned off. A warm boot involves restarting the system while it is running …
got booted from the game
was booted out of the office
booted home three winners
boot a computer
the program boots automatically
the computer boots quickly

data.text: a navy or marine corps camp for basic training
examples: Nowadays, the more talented players view their college years as necessary boot camp to prepare them for the riches waiting for them in pro football.
These youngsters are put through what amounts to a boot camp of courses in music notation, basic harmony and ear-training.

data.text: a disciplinary facility or program in which young offenders are forced to participate in a rigidly structured routine
examples: Nowadays, the more talented players view their college years as necessary boot camp to prepare them for the riches waiting for them in pro football.
These youngsters are put through what amounts to a boot camp of courses in music notation, basic harmony and ear-training.

data.text: a place or undertaking that resembles a military boot camp especially by requiring one to endure intensive training or initiation
examples: Nowadays, the more talented players view their college years as necessary boot camp to prepare them for the riches waiting for them in pro football.
These youngsters are put through what amounts to a boot camp of courses in music notation, basic harmony and ear-training.

data.text: a boot made with a high arch, a high Cuban heel, and usually fancy stitching
a metal clamp that is locked onto one of the wheels of an automobile to immobilize it especially until its owner pays accumulated parking fines
a boot with a top reaching above the ankle and ending below the knee
